subject: Gaming
title: Space Invaders Changes Everything
description: One-minute skill: Space Invaders Changes Everything.

hook.text: Space Invaders was so popular in Japan that it caused a national coin shortage in 1978.

buildup.text: Tomohiro Nishikado designed and programmed the entire game alone. It took him a full year.

discovery.text: The aliens speed up as you destroy them—not by design. The processor ran faster with fewer sprites on screen.

twist.text: That accidental speed increase created gaming's first difficulty curve. A bug became a beloved feature.

climax.text: Space Invaders earned $3.8 billion in quarters by 1982—more than Star Wars earned at the box office.

punchline.text: A coin shortage and a happy bug. Gaming's first blockbuster.

quiz.question: Why did the aliens speed up as you destroyed them?
options: The processor ran faster with fewer sprites on screen
It was a deliberate design decision
